framing: Senior powertrain engineer at BMW applying to Rivian's new Germany engineering office

jd.title: Staff Battery Systems Engineer
jd.style_note: Startup-technical: specific EV technology, fast-paced, mission-driven, deep technical requirements

requirements.text: 8+ years of experience in battery systems engineering, battery management systems (BMS), or high-voltage powertrain development
Expert knowledge of lithium-ion cell chemistries (NMC, LFP) and cell-to-pack architectures
Experience with BMS software development: cell balancing algorithms, SOC/SOH estimation, thermal management
Proficiency in MATLAB/Simulink for battery system modelling and HIL/SIL testing
Experience with automotive functional safety (ISO 26262) for high-voltage systems
Familiarity with CAN/CAN-FD and automotive communication protocols
Experience with battery test equipment: Bitrode, Arbin, or Maccor cyclers
Knowledge of UN ECE R100 and EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) compliance

responsibilities: Lead battery pack system design for next-generation Rivian platform (800V architecture)
Develop and validate BMS algorithms for thermal management, cell balancing, and state estimation
Collaborate with cell suppliers (Samsung SDI, CATL) on cell characterisation and incoming quality
Drive FMEA and functional safety analysis (ISO 26262 ASIL-D) for battery system
Mentor team of 3 battery engineers at Germany engineering office

expected_output.summary: Powertrain engineer with 10 years of experience at BMW Group, most recently Senior Engineer in the eDrive division working on high-voltage battery systems for the BMW iX and i4 platforms. Deep expertise in 400V battery pack integration, thermal management system design, and MATLAB/Simulink-based powertrain modelling. Experience with ISO 26262 functional safety up to ASIL-C. Led battery pack integration for 2 production vehicle programmes. Seeking transition from OEM to EV-native company.

experience.title: Senior Engineer — eDrive Battery Systems
bullets: Led battery pack thermal management system design for BMW iX (Gen5 eDrive, 400V, 111.5 kWh NMC cells)
Developed MATLAB/Simulink models for battery thermal behaviour validated against dyno test data (>95% correlation)
Performed FMEA and ISO 26262 functional safety analysis (ASIL-C) for battery disconnect unit and contactors
Collaborated with Samsung SDI and CATL on cell characterisation, incoming quality specs, and ageing models
Managed CAN communication interface specifications between BMS and vehicle control units

experience.title: Development Engineer — Electric Powertrain (promoted from Junior Engineer, 2018)
bullets: Supported battery pack integration for BMW i3 facelift (120 Ah cell upgrade) and BMW iX3
Built HIL test environments for BMS software validation using dSPACE MicroAutoBox
Conducted high-voltage safety testing according to UN ECE R100 homologation requirements
Performed battery pack abuse testing (nail penetration, thermal runaway propagation) at BMW's Aschheim test centre
Completed BMW's internal electric mobility specialist training programme

certifications: ISO 26262 Functional Safety Professional — TÜV Rheinland (2022)
education: MSc Electrical Engineering — Technical University of Munich (TUM) (2013-2015)
BSc Electrical and Electronic Engineering — Technical University of Munich (TUM) (2010-2013)

jd_requirements: 8+ years battery systems BMS high-voltage powertrain
BMS software cell balancing SOC SOH thermal management
MATLAB Simulink battery modelling HIL SIL
Bitrode Arbin Maccor battery test cyclers
UN ECE R100 EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542
deliberate_flaws: No 800V architecture experience — BMW iX/i4 are 400V platforms (Rivian wants 800V)
No LFP cell chemistry experience — BMW uses NMC (JD wants both NMC and LFP knowledge)
No cell-to-pack (CTP) architecture experience — BMW uses cell-to-module (CTM)
ISO 26262 experience is ASIL-C, not ASIL-D (JD requires ASIL-D for battery system)
No BMS software development experience — thermal management system design, not BMS algorithms
No SOC/SOH estimation algorithm development (used BMW's proprietary BMS, didn't develop algorithms)
No Bitrode, Arbin, or Maccor cycler experience (used BMW's in-house test equipment)
No EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) compliance experience (regulation is newer than his BMW work)
OEM to startup transition — BMW's structured processes vs Rivian's fast-paced environment
No SIL testing experience (only HIL with dSPACE)

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
